[지문 50]

A Letter to My Future Self

Today in class, our teacher asked us to write a letter to our future selves. We had to write about our current life, our friends, our dreams, and our worries. We then put the letters in a time capsule, which we will open on our graduation day in three years. In my letter, I wrote about my dream of becoming a veterinarian because I love animals. I wrote about my best friend, Sora, and the funny things we do together. I also wrote about my worry that I'm not good enough at math. I asked my future self: "Did you get into a good university? Are you still friends with Sora? Did you get a puppy?" It was a strange but interesting experience. I wonder how much I will have changed in three years.

Vocabulary

  • current 현재의
  • time capsule 타임캡슐
  • graduation 졸업
  • veterinarian 수의사
  • worry 걱정
50-1 Question

What did the writer do in class?

  1. He wrote a letter to his teacher.
  2. He wrote a letter to his best friend.
  3. He wrote a letter to his future self.
  4. He made a time capsule.
  5. He gave a presentation about his dream.
50-2 Question

Which of the following did the writer NOT write about in his letter?

  1. His dream of becoming a veterinarian
  2. His best friend, Sora
  3. His favorite food
  4. His worry about math
  5. Questions for his future self
50-3 Question

When will the writer open the time capsule?

  1. Next week
  2. Next year
  3. On his birthday
  4. On graduation day
  5. He will never open it.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 51]

The World of Sign Language

Have you ever seen people talking with their hands? They are likely using sign language. Sign language is a complete language that uses hand gestures, facial expressions, and body movements to communicate. It is not a universal language; different countries have their own sign languages, just like spoken languages. For example, American Sign Language (ASL) is different from British Sign Language (BSL). For many deaf and hard-of-hearing people, sign language is their primary way of communication. Learning sign language can be a wonderful experience. It allows you to communicate with a new community of people. It also helps you become more aware of the diverse ways people interact with the world.

Vocabulary

  • gesture 몸짓, 제스처
  • facial expression 얼굴 표정
  • communicate 의사소통하다
  • universal 보편적인, 세계적인
  • interact 소통하다, 교류하다
51-1 Question

What is sign language?

  1. A secret code used by spies
  2. A universal language that everyone knows
  3. A language that uses hands, face, and body to communicate
  4. A simple way to say "hello" and "goodbye"
  5. A written language for deaf people
51-2 Question

According to the passage, which statement is TRUE?

  1. Sign language is the same all over the world.
  2. Only deaf people can learn sign language.
  3. American Sign Language and British Sign Language are identical.
  4. Sign language uses only hand gestures.
  5. Different countries have their own sign languages.
51-3 Question

위 글에서 수화를 배우는 것의 장점으로 언급되지 않은 것은?

  1. 새로운 공동체의 사람들과 의사소통할 수 있다.
  2. 사람들이 세상을 살아가는 다양한 방식에 대해 더 잘 알게 된다.
  3. 더 좋은 직업을 쉽게 구할 수 있다.
  4. 청각 장애를 가진 사람들과 소통하는 데 도움이 된다.
  5. 손과 얼굴 표정을 사용하는 새로운 언어를 배우게 된다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 52]

A Camping Trip Gone Wrong

My family loves camping. Last summer, we went to a new campsite in the mountains. We set up our tent and were excited for a night under the stars. However, the weather forecast was wrong. Suddenly, dark clouds gathered, and it started to rain heavily. The wind was so strong that our tent almost blew away. We were all huddled inside, listening to the thunder. The rain leaked into our tent, and our sleeping bags got wet. We couldn't make a campfire or cook our dinner. We had to eat cold sandwiches. It was not the fun camping trip we had expected. But looking back, it was a memorable adventure. It taught us to always be prepared for unexpected situations.

Vocabulary

  • campsite 야영지, 캠핑장
  • forecast 예보
  • huddle 옹기종기 모이다
  • leak (물, 빛 등이) 새다
  • expect 기대하다, 예상하다
  • adventure 모험
52-1 Question

What was the main problem during the camping trip?

  1. They forgot to bring their tent.
  2. They got lost in the mountains.
  3. The weather suddenly turned bad.
  4. They saw a wild animal.
  5. They ran out of food.
52-2 Question

What did the family have for dinner?

  1. Hot dogs cooked over a campfire
  2. Barbecue
  3. Fish they caught from the river
  4. Cold sandwiches
  5. They skipped dinner.
52-3 Question

이 경험을 통해 글쓴이의 가족이 배운 교훈은 무엇인가?

  1. 캠핑은 항상 재미있다.
  2. 산에서는 날씨가 변하지 않는다.
  3. 예상치 못한 상황에 항상 대비해야 한다.
  4. 샌드위치는 최고의 캠핑 음식이다.
  5. 캠핑은 다시는 가지 말아야 한다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 53]

The Great Wall of China

The Great Wall of China is one of the most impressive structures ever built by humans. It is not a single wall but a series of walls and fortifications. It stretches for thousands of kilometers across northern China. The main purpose of the wall was to protect China from invasions by nomadic tribes from the north. The construction of the wall started over 2,000 years ago and continued for centuries. Millions of soldiers and common people worked to build it. The wall was built using various materials, including stone, brick, and earth. Today, the Great Wall is a famous tourist attraction. It is a symbol of China's long history and the perseverance of its people.

Vocabulary

  • structure 구조물
  • fortification 요새, 방어 시설
  • purpose 목적
  • invasion 침략, 침입
  • attraction 명소, 명물
  • perseverance 인내, 끈기
53-1 Question

What was the main purpose of the Great Wall of China?

  1. To be a tourist attraction
  2. To protect China from invasions
  3. To be a road for trade
  4. To mark the center of the country
  5. To show the emperor's power
53-2 Question

Which statement about the Great Wall is NOT true?

  1. It is a single, continuous wall.
  2. It stretches for thousands of kilometers.
  3. It was built over many centuries.
  4. Millions of people worked to build it.
  5. It is now a famous tourist site.
53-3 Question

위 글을 통해 만리장성이 상징하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  1. 중국의 빠른 경제 발전
  2. 중국의 아름다운 자연
  3. 중국의 오랜 역사와 인내심
  4. 북방 유목민과의 평화
  5. 현대 건축 기술의 우수성
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 54]

Why Do We Yawn?

Everyone yawns. You might yawn when you are tired or bored. But have you ever wondered why we do it? For a long time, scientists believed that we yawn to get more oxygen into our blood. However, recent studies suggest a different reason. The main theory now is that yawning helps to cool down our brain. Your brain works best when it is at a certain temperature. When your brain gets too warm, a yawn can help bring cooler blood to it. This might explain why we yawn more when we are tired, as tiredness can raise brain temperature. It also explains why yawning is contagious. Seeing someone else yawn might be a signal to our brain that it's time to cool down too.

Vocabulary

  • yawn 하품하다
  • oxygen 산소
  • cool down 식히다
  • temperature 온도
  • contagious 전염성이 있는
54-1 Question

What is the new theory about why we yawn?

  1. To get more oxygen
  2. To show that we are bored
  3. To exercise our jaw muscles
  4. To cool down our brain
  5. To wake ourselves up
54-2 Question

The word "contagious" in the passage means:

  1. something that is very interesting.
  2. something that is easily spread from one person to another.
  3. something that is difficult to understand.
  4. something that is good for your health.
  5. something that happens only when you are tired.
54-3 Question

위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  1. 하품은 혈액 속 산소를 부족하게 만든다.
  2. 뇌가 너무 차가워질 때 하품을 한다.
  3. 하품이 전염되는 이유는 아직 아무도 모른다.
  4. 피곤할 때 뇌의 온도가 올라갈 수 있다.
  5. 하품의 원인에 대한 과학적 이론은 변한 적이 없다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 55]

A Visit to a Traditional Market

Last weekend, I went to a traditional market with my grandmother. It was very different from the clean, quiet supermarket I usually go to. The market was crowded and noisy, but it was full of life and energy. The air was filled with the smells of fresh vegetables, fried chicken, and various spices. Vendors were shouting to attract customers. My grandmother seemed to know everyone. She greeted many of the shop owners. She taught me how to pick the freshest fish and how to bargain for a better price. We bought a lot of things and our hands were full. It was a fun experience. The traditional market is not just a place to shop; it's a place full of human connection and culture.

Vocabulary

  • vendor 노점상, 행상인
  • spice 향신료, 양념
  • bargain 흥정하다
  • connection 연결, 관계
55-1 Question

How was the traditional market different from a supermarket?

  1. It was quiet and empty.
  2. It was crowded, noisy, and full of energy.
  3. It only sold packaged goods.
  4. It was cleaner and more modern.
  5. The prices were fixed.
55-2 Question

What did the writer's grandmother teach him at the market?

  1. How to cook fish
  2. How to greet people in different languages
  3. How to pick fresh fish and bargain
  4. How to find the exit
  5. How to avoid the crowds
55-3 Question

위 글에서 글쓴이가 생각하는 재래시장의 가장 큰 특징은 무엇인가?

  1. 물건 값이 매우 싸다는 점
  2. 원하는 물건을 찾기 쉽다는 점
  3. 쇼핑하기에 매우 편리하다는 점
  4. 사람 사이의 정과 문화를 느낄 수 있는 장소라는 점
  5. 항상 조용하고 깨끗하다는 점
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 56]

The Life of a Butterfly

The life of a butterfly is a story of amazing transformation. This process is called metamorphosis. A butterfly's life has four stages. First, a female butterfly lays her eggs on a leaf or stem. This is the egg stage. Second, a larva, which we call a caterpillar, hatches from the egg. The caterpillar's main job is to eat and grow. It eats a lot and grows very quickly. Third, when the caterpillar is big enough, it forms a protective shell around itself called a chrysalis (or pupa). This is the pupa stage. Inside the chrysalis, an incredible change happens. Finally, a beautiful butterfly emerges from the chrysalis. It is ready to fly, drink nectar, and start the cycle all over again.

Vocabulary

  • transformation 변화, 변신
  • metamorphosis 탈바꿈, 변태
  • stage 단계
  • larva 애벌레, 유충
  • emerge 나오다, 나타나다
56-1 Question

What is the correct order of a butterfly's life stages?

  1. Egg → Pupa → Larva → Butterfly
  2. Larva → Egg → Pupa → Butterfly
  3. Egg → Larva → Pupa → Butterfly
  4. Pupa → Egg → Larva → Butterfly
  5. Egg → Butterfly → Larva → Pupa
56-2 Question

What is the main job of a caterpillar?

  1. To fly and find flowers
  2. To lay eggs
  3. To eat and grow
  4. To form a chrysalis immediately
  5. To sleep all day
56-3 Question

위 글에서 '번데기' 단계를 영어로 무엇이라고 하는가?

  1. Egg
  2. Larva
  3. Caterpillar
  4. Pupa
  5. Butterfly
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 57]

The Lost Dog

One afternoon, a little girl named Lily was playing in her yard when she saw a small dog. The dog looked lost and scared. It had a collar but no name tag. Lily felt sorry for the dog. She slowly approached it and gave it some water. The dog seemed friendly. Lily's mom helped her make some "Lost Dog" posters. They put the dog's picture on the poster and their phone number. They put the posters up all around their neighborhood. The next day, they got a call. It was from an elderly man who lived a few blocks away. He was so happy and relieved. His dog, Sparky, had run out of the house the day before. Lily was sad to say goodbye to Sparky, but she was happy that she could help him find his way home.

Vocabulary

  • collar 목걸이, 칼라
  • approach 다가가다, 접근하다
  • neighborhood 동네, 이웃
  • elderly 나이가 지긋한
  • relieved 안도하는
57-1 Question

What did Lily do to find the dog's owner?

  1. She called the police.
  2. She took the dog to an animal shelter.
  3. She made and put up "Lost Dog" posters.
  4. She walked around the city with the dog.
  5. She posted about the dog on the internet.
57-2 Question

Who was the owner of the lost dog?

  1. A young boy
  2. A police officer
  3. An elderly man
  4. Lily's neighbor
  5. The poster didn't work, so they don't know.
57-3 Question

How did Lily feel at the end of the story?

  1. Angry that she had to give the dog back
  2. Sad to say goodbye, but happy to have helped
  3. Happy to keep the dog forever
  4. Disappointed that the owner was found
  5. Worried that the dog would get lost again
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 58]

The Benefits of Reading Newspapers

In this digital age, many people get their news from the internet or social media. However, reading a physical newspaper still has many benefits. First, newspapers are written by professional journalists and checked by editors, so the information is generally more reliable than what you find on social media. Second, reading a newspaper can improve your vocabulary and reading comprehension skills. You are exposed to a wide range of topics and writing styles. Third, it helps you understand the world better. You can learn about politics, economics, culture, and sports all in one place. It can make you a more informed citizen. So, try picking up a newspaper once in a while.

Vocabulary

  • benefit 이점, 혜택
  • journalist 기자
  • reliable 신뢰할 수 있는, 믿을 만한
  • comprehension 이해력
  • citizen 시민
58-1 Question

What is the main point of the passage?

  1. The internet is the best source of news.
  2. Reading newspapers has several benefits.
  3. How to become a journalist.
  4. The history of newspapers.
  5. The problems with social media.
58-2 Question

According to the passage, why is newspaper information often more reliable?

  1. It is written by famous people.
  2. It is checked by professional journalists and editors.
  3. It is free for everyone.
  4. It has more pictures.
  5. It is updated every minute.
58-3 Question

위 글에서 신문을 읽는 것의 장점으로 언급되지 않은 것은?

  1. 어휘력과 독해력을 향상시킨다.
  2. 세상을 더 잘 이해하게 도와준다.
  3. 더 많은 정보를 가진 시민이 되게 한다.
  4. 소셜 미디어보다 더 빨리 소식을 접할 수 있다.
  5. 신뢰할 만한 정보를 얻을 수 있다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 59]

The Mystery of the Bermuda Triangle

The Bermuda Triangle, also known as the Devil's Triangle, is a region in the western part of the North Atlantic Ocean. It is famous for a mystery: many ships and airplanes have reportedly disappeared there under mysterious circumstances. Some people have proposed strange explanations. They blame aliens, giant sea monsters, or portals to another dimension. However, scientists and experts offer more logical explanations. They point out that this area has very bad weather, including many hurricanes. The ocean floor in this region is also very deep, making it hard to find any wreckage. Furthermore, many of the stories of disappearances have been exaggerated or are not accurate. While the mystery is fun to think about, the reality is likely less supernatural.

Vocabulary

  • region 지역, 지방
  • mystery 미스터리, 수수께끼
  • disappear 사라지다
  • exaggerate 과장하다
  • reality 현실, 실제
  • supernatural 초자연적인
59-1 Question

What is the Bermuda Triangle famous for?

  1. Its beautiful beaches
  2. The mysterious disappearances of ships and planes
  3. Its calm and safe weather
  4. Its unique sea animals
  5. A portal to another dimension
59-2 Question

Which of the following is a logical explanation for the disappearances?

  1. Alien abductions
  2. Attacks by sea monsters
  3. Bad weather like hurricanes
  4. A secret government base
  5. The ships and planes were invisible.
59-3 Question

위 글에 따르면, 버뮤다 삼각지대에 대한 작가의 생각은 무엇인가?

  1. 외계인이나 괴물과 같은 초자연적인 현상이 원인일 것이다.
  2. 미스터리는 재미있지만, 실제 원인은 악천후 등 현실적인 이유일 가능성이 높다.
  3. 버뮤다 삼각지대는 세상에서 가장 위험한 곳이므로 가서는 안 된다.
  4. 사라진 배와 비행기에 대한 모든 이야기는 사실이다.
  5. 버뮤다 삼각지대의 미스터리는 영원히 풀리지 않을 것이다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 60]

How to Make a Good First Impression

They say you never get a second chance to make a first impression. A first impression is the feeling or opinion someone forms about you when they meet you for the first time. It happens very quickly. So, how can you make a good one? First, smile. A genuine smile makes you look friendly and approachable. Second, make eye contact. It shows that you are confident and paying attention. Third, have good posture. Stand up straight and don't slouch. It makes you look more self-assured. Finally, be a good listener. Ask questions and show interest in the other person. People appreciate it when you listen to them. These simple things can help you start any new relationship on a positive note.

Vocabulary

  • impression 인상
  • genuine 진짜의, 진실한
  • approachable 다가가기 쉬운, 친근한
  • posture 자세
  • slouch 구부정하게 서다/앉다
60-1 Question

What is a "first impression"?

  1. The last thing you remember about someone
  2. A detailed report about a person
  3. The feeling someone forms about you when they first meet you
  4. A test you have to pass to make a friend
  5. A gift you give to a new person
60-2 Question

Which is NOT a tip for making a good first impression mentioned in the passage?

  1. Smile
  2. Make eye contact
  3. Talk a lot about yourself
  4. Have good posture
  5. Be a good listener
60-3 Question

위 글에서 좋은 자세를 취하는 것이 왜 중요하다고 말하는가?

  1. 키가 더 커 보이기 때문에
  2. 더 자신감 있어 보이기 때문에
  3. 다른 사람을 존중하는 것처럼 보이기 때문에
  4. 옷이 더 잘 어울려 보이기 때문에
  5. 더 똑똑해 보이기 때문에
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 61]

The Trojan Horse

The story of the Trojan Horse comes from the ancient Greek legend of the Trojan War. The Greeks had been fighting the city of Troy for ten years, but they could not break through its strong walls. So, the clever Greek hero Odysseus came up with a plan. The Greeks built a giant wooden horse and left it outside the gates of Troy. The Trojans thought it was a victory gift from the gods and pulled it into their city. They celebrated all night. But the horse was not empty. It was filled with Greek soldiers. That night, while the Trojans were asleep, the soldiers climbed out, opened the city gates for the rest of the Greek army, and conquered the city. The story is a famous symbol of using cleverness to defeat an enemy.

Vocabulary

  • legend 전설
  • victory 승리
  • celebrate 축하하다, 기념하다
  • conquer 정복하다
  • symbol 상징
61-1 Question

What was the Trojan Horse?

  1. A real horse that could talk
  2. A gift from the Trojans to the Greeks
  3. A giant wooden horse filled with Greek soldiers
  4. A statue of the Greek hero Odysseus
  5. A new type of weapon
61-2 Question

Why did the Greeks build the Trojan Horse?

  1. To show their respect for the Trojans
  2. To trick the Trojans and get inside their city
  3. To use it as a boat to go home
  4. To offer it as a sacrifice to the gods
  5. To use it as a watchtower
61-3 Question

위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  1. 그리스인들은 10년 동안 트로이와 전쟁을 했다.
  2. 트로이인들은 목마를 승리의 선물이라고 생각했다.
  3. 목마 안에는 아무도 없었다.
  4. 그리스 병사들은 밤에 목마에서 나와 성문을 열었다.
  5. 이 이야기는 지혜를 사용해 적을 이기는 것의 상징이다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 62]

The Importance of Water

Water is essential for all life on Earth. Our bodies are about 60% water. We need water to digest food, carry nutrients, and control our body temperature. You can survive for weeks without food, but only a few days without water. However, many people take water for granted. We use it for drinking, cooking, cleaning, and growing food. But fresh, clean water is a limited resource. In some parts of the world, people suffer from water shortages. We must learn to conserve water. Simple actions like turning off the tap while brushing your teeth, taking shorter showers, and fixing leaky faucets can save a lot of water. Every drop counts.

Vocabulary

  • digest 소화하다
  • take for granted ~을 당연하게 여기다
  • limited 한정된, 제한된
  • shortage 부족, 결핍
  • conserve 아끼다, 보존하다
  • count 중요하다, 가치가 있다
62-1 Question

What is the main message of the passage?

  1. Water is a beautiful part of nature.
  2. Our bodies are mostly made of water.
  3. Water is essential, and we must conserve it.
  4. Many people in the world have enough water.
  5. How to find clean water.
62-2 Question

Which is NOT a way to conserve water mentioned in the passage?

  1. Taking shorter showers
  2. Fixing leaky faucets
  3. Turning off the tap while brushing teeth
  4. Drinking bottled water instead of tap water
  5. Being mindful of water usage
62-3 Question

위 글에 따르면, 우리 몸에서 물이 하는 역할이 아닌 것은?

  1. 음식 소화시키기
  2. 영양분 운반하기
  3. 체온 조절하기
  4. 뼈를 튼튼하게 만들기
  5. 생명 유지에 필수적인 역할 하기
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 63]

A School Talent Show

Our school held a talent show last Friday. Many students showed off their unique talents. There were singers, dancers, magicians, and even a comedian. I decided to participate with my band. We have been practicing a rock song for months. When it was our turn to go on stage, my heart was pounding. The lights were so bright, and I could see hundreds of faces in the audience. I was the lead singer. I took a deep breath and started to sing. At first, I was nervous, but then I saw my friends cheering for me. I started to enjoy the moment. We played our song with all our energy. The audience clapped along to the music. Even though we didn't win the first prize, it was an amazing experience. The most important thing was sharing our passion with others.

Vocabulary

  • talent 재능
  • comedian 코미디언, 희극 배우
  • participate 참가하다
  • pound (심장이) 두근거리다
  • passion 열정
63-1 Question

What did the writer do in the talent show?

  1. He told jokes.
  2. He performed magic tricks.
  3. He danced.
  4. He sang in a band.
  5. He played the piano.
63-2 Question

How did the writer feel before going on stage?

  1. Calm and relaxed
  2. Bored and tired
  3. Nervous and excited
  4. Angry and upset
  5. Confident and proud
63-3 Question

이 글을 통해 글쓴이가 가장 중요하게 생각하는 것은 무엇인가?

  1. 장기자랑에서 1등 상을 타는 것
  2. 무대 위에서 실수하지 않는 것
  3. 다른 사람들보다 더 뛰어난 재능을 보여주는 것
  4. 자신의 열정을 다른 사람들과 나누는 것
  5. 많은 관객들 앞에서 유명해지는 것
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 64]

The Amazing Octopus

The octopus is one of the most intelligent and fascinating creatures in the ocean. It has a soft body and eight long arms, called tentacles. Each tentacle is covered with suckers that can taste and touch. One of the octopus's most amazing abilities is its skill at camouflage. It can change its skin color and texture in an instant to blend in with its surroundings, like rocks or coral. This helps it hide from predators and ambush its prey. An octopus also has three hearts and blue blood. If it is in danger, it can squirt a cloud of black ink to confuse its enemy and make a quick escape. It is a true master of disguise and survival.

Vocabulary

  • tentacle 촉수
  • sucker 빨판
  • ability 능력
  • camouflage 위장
  • disguise 변장, 위장
64-1 Question

What is one of the octopus's most amazing abilities?

  1. Singing beautiful songs
  2. Changing its skin color and texture
  3. Building nests with sand
  4. Flying for a short distance
  5. Talking to other fish
64-2 Question

What does an octopus do when it is in danger?

  1. It plays dead.
  2. It squirts a cloud of black ink.
  3. It bites its enemy with sharp teeth.
  4. It hides inside its shell.
  5. It swims faster than any other creature.
64-3 Question

위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  1. 문어는 부드러운 몸과 8개의 팔을 가졌다.
  2. 문어는 주변 환경에 맞춰 피부색을 바꿀 수 있다.
  3. 문어는 심장이 한 개이고 피는 붉은색이다.
  4. 문어의 팔에는 맛을 보고 만질 수 있는 빨판이 있다.
  5. 문어는 위장과 생존의 대가이다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 65]

A Pen Pal from Another Country

My English teacher suggested that we find a pen pal to practice our English. A pen pal is someone you write letters to regularly, often someone who lives far away. I was matched with a boy named Leo from Australia. I wrote my first letter, telling him about my school, my family, and my hobbies. A few weeks later, I received a letter back from him. He told me about his life in Sydney. He loves surfing and has a pet koala living in his backyard (just kidding, he said koalas are wild animals). We have been writing to each other for six months now. We share photos and small gifts. It's so interesting to learn about a different culture and life. Having a pen pal has not only improved my English but also given me a new friend from the other side of the world.

Vocabulary

  • pen pal 펜팔 친구
  • regularly 정기적으로
  • receive 받다
  • backyard 뒷마당
  • culture 문화
65-1 Question

What is a pen pal?

  1. A friend who lives next door
  2. A person you write letters to regularly
  3. A character in a book
  4. A type of pen used for writing letters
  5. A teacher who teaches English
65-2 Question

Where is the writer's pen pal from?

  1. America
  2. England
  3. Canada
  4. Australia
  5. New Zealand
65-3 Question

펜팔을 통해 글쓴이가 얻은 것이 아닌 것은?

  1. 영어 실력 향상
  2. 다른 문화에 대한 이해
  3. 세상 반대편에 있는 새로운 친구
  4. 호주로 여행 갈 수 있는 기회
  5. 편지와 사진을 통한 즐거운 교류
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 66]

The Invention of the Telephone

Before the telephone was invented, long-distance communication was slow. People had to send letters, which could take weeks or months to arrive. Or they used the telegraph, which could only send messages in code. This all changed thanks to an inventor named Alexander Graham Bell. Bell was working on a way to send human voice over wires. On March 10, 1876, he made the first successful telephone call. He was in one room, and his assistant, Thomas Watson, was in another. Bell spoke into the device, "Mr. Watson, come here. I want to see you." Watson heard the words clearly. This invention completely changed the world. It made communication faster and brought people closer together.

Vocabulary

  • communication 의사소통, 통신
  • telegraph 전신기
  • inventor 발명가
  • assistant 조수, 보조원
  • device 장치
  • invention 발명, 발명품
66-1 Question

Who is credited with inventing the telephone?

  1. Thomas Edison
  2. Thomas Watson
  3. Alexander Graham Bell
  4. Samuel Morse
  5. The Wright brothers
66-2 Question

What were the first words spoken on the telephone?

  1. "Hello, can you hear me?"
  2. "This is a great invention."
  3. "Mr. Watson, come here. I want to see you."
  4. "One small step for man."
  5. "What hath God wrought?"
66-3 Question

전화기가 발명되기 전, 사람들이 장거리 소통을 위해 사용했던 방법은?

  1. 이메일과 문자 메시지
  2. 편지와 전보
  3. 비둘기를 이용한 통신
  4. 소리를 질러서 전달하기
  5. 스마트폰 앱
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 67]

A Day at the Amusement Park

Last Saturday, my friends and I went to an amusement park. I love the thrill of roller coasters. The first ride we went on was the "Mega Drop." It takes you high up into the sky and then suddenly drops you. I screamed my head off, but it was so much fun. After that, we rode a looping roller coaster and got dizzy. We also enjoyed some classic rides like the Ferris wheel and the bumper cars. From the top of the Ferris wheel, we could see the entire park. It was a beautiful sight. We ate cotton candy and popcorn. The day went by so fast. We left the park feeling tired but extremely happy. It was a perfect day to forget about school and just have fun.

Vocabulary

  • amusement park 놀이공원
  • thrill 스릴, 흥분
  • scream 소리 지르다
  • dizzy 어지러운
  • classic 고전적인, 전형적인
67-1 Question

What is the writer's favorite thing about amusement parks?

  1. The food
  2. The parades
  3. The thrill of roller coasters
  4. The water rides
  5. The souvenir shops
67-2 Question

Which ride gave the writer a beautiful view of the entire park?

  1. The roller coaster
  2. The Mega Drop
  3. The bumper cars
  4. The Ferris wheel
  5. The haunted house
67-3 Question

위 글에 나타난 글쓴이의 기분으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  1. calm and peaceful
  2. sad and disappointed
  3. excited and happy
  4. scared and worried
  5. bored and sleepy
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 68]

The Problem of Plastic Pollution

Plastic is a very useful material. It's cheap, strong, and can be made into almost anything. Look around you. You will see plastic in your phone, your clothes, and your water bottle. However, our love for plastic has created a huge problem: pollution. Most plastic is not biodegradable, which means it does not break down naturally. It stays in the environment for hundreds of years. Plastic waste ends up in our oceans, harming marine animals. They can mistake plastic bags for food or get tangled in plastic rings. We need to reduce our use of plastic. We can use reusable bags, water bottles, and coffee cups. We must also recycle plastic properly. Every small action helps protect our planet.

Vocabulary

  • material 재료, 물질
  • biodegradable 생분해성의 (자연적으로 분해되는)
  • tangled 얽힌
  • reduce 줄이다
  • reusable 재사용할 수 있는
68-1 Question

What is the main problem with plastic mentioned in the passage?

  1. It is too expensive.
  2. It is not strong enough.
  3. It is not biodegradable and causes pollution.
  4. It is difficult to make.
  5. There is not enough plastic in the world.
68-2 Question

How does plastic waste harm marine animals?

  1. They like the taste of plastic.
  2. It makes the ocean water warmer.
  3. They can mistake it for food or get tangled in it.
  4. It makes it hard for them to see.
  5. It cleans the ocean.
68-3 Question

이 글에서 플라스틱 사용을 줄이기 위한 방법으로 제안되지 않은 것은?

  1. 재사용 가능한 가방 사용하기
  2. 재사용 가능한 물병 사용하기
  3. 플라스틱을 올바르게 재활용하기
  4. 플라스틱 제품을 전혀 사용하지 않기
  5. 재사용 가능한 커피컵 사용하기
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 69]

A Total Mess

My parents went on a trip for the weekend, leaving me home alone for the first time. I was excited. I invited my best friend, Mark, over. We decided to bake a cake. But we are not good bakers. We spilled flour all over the kitchen floor. We cracked an egg, and it dripped down the side of the counter. The cake itself got burnt in the oven and filled the house with smoke. The kitchen was a total mess. We looked at each other and just started laughing. It was a disaster, but a funny one. We spent the next two hours cleaning everything up. We didn't get to eat any cake, but we definitely learned a lesson: follow the recipe carefully!

Vocabulary

  • spill 엎지르다, 흘리다
  • drip 뚝뚝 떨어지다
  • burnt (불에) 탄
  • mess 엉망, 뒤죽박죽
  • disaster 재앙, 큰 실패
  • recipe 요리법, 레시피
69-1 Question

What did the writer and his friend try to do?

  1. Clean the house
  2. Watch a movie
  3. Bake a cake
  4. Do their homework
  5. Fix the oven
69-2 Question

Which of the following did NOT happen in the kitchen?

  1. Flour was spilled on the floor.
  2. An egg dripped down the counter.
  3. The cake was burnt.
  4. The fire alarm went off.
  5. The house was filled with smoke.
69-3 Question

이 경험을 통해 글쓴이가 배운 교훈은 무엇인가?

  1. 부모님 없이는 아무것도 할 수 없다.
  2. 케이크를 굽는 것은 매우 쉽다.
  3. 요리법을 잘 따라야 한다.
  4. 친구와는 함께 요리하면 안 된다.
  5. 어지르는 것은 재미있다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 70]

The Five Senses

We experience the world through our five senses: sight, hearing, smell, taste, and touch. Our eyes give us the sense of sight, allowing us to see colors, shapes, and movements. Our ears give us the sense of hearing, letting us hear music, voices, and all the sounds around us. Our nose gives us the sense of smell, which can warn us of danger (like smoke) or bring us pleasure (like the smell of flowers). Our tongue gives us the sense of taste, so we can enjoy different flavors like sweet, sour, salty, and bitter. Our skin gives us the sense of touch, allowing us to feel textures like soft or rough, and temperatures like hot or cold. These five senses work together to give us a complete picture of the world.

Vocabulary

  • sense 감각
  • sight 시각
  • hearing 청각
  • smell 후각
  • taste 미각
  • touch 촉각
70-1 Question

What is the main topic of the passage?

  1. The five senses and their functions
  2. The importance of the sense of sight
  3. How our brain processes information
  4. The different flavors we can taste
  5. How to protect our senses
70-2 Question

Which sense can warn us of danger like smoke?

  1. Sight
  2. Hearing
  3. Smell
  4. Taste
  5. Touch
70-3 Question

위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  1. 우리는 세상을 여섯 가지 감각으로 경험한다.
  2. 귀는 우리에게 미각을 제공한다.
  3. 피부는 우리에게 촉각을 느끼게 해준다.
  4. 혀는 냄새를 맡는 역할을 한다.
  5. 다섯 가지 감각은 각각 독립적으로 작용한다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 71]

The Art of Mime

A mime is a type of performer who tells a story without using words. They use body movements, gestures, and facial expressions to communicate their ideas and emotions. A famous mime act is pretending to be trapped in an invisible box. The mime uses their hands to show the walls of the box, creating the illusion for the audience. Mimes often wear white face paint to make their facial expressions clearer. They also typically wear black and white clothing to help the audience focus on their movements. Becoming a mime requires great physical control and imagination. It is a silent art form that can be both funny and touching.

Vocabulary

  • performer 공연자, 연기자
  • invisible 보이지 않는
  • illusion 환상, 착각
  • typically 보통, 일반적으로
  • imagination 상상력
71-1 Question

How does a mime tell a story?

  1. By singing songs
  2. By writing on a board
  3. By using body movements and facial expressions
  4. By using puppets
  5. By speaking very softly
71-2 Question

Why do mimes often wear white face paint?

  1. To look scary
  2. To make their facial expressions clearer
  3. Because it is a tradition with no reason
  4. To protect their skin from the sun
  5. To look like a clown
71-3 Question

위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  1. 마임은 말을 사용하지 않고 이야기를 전달하는 공연자이다.
  2. 보이지 않는 상자 안에 갇힌 척하는 것은 유명한 마임 연기이다.
  3. 마임 배우가 되려면 뛰어난 신체 조절 능력이 필요하다.
  4. 마임은 항상 재미있기만 한 예술 형식이다.
  5. 마임 배우는 보통 흑백 의상을 입는다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 72]

The Greenhouse Effect

The greenhouse effect is a natural process that warms the Earth's surface. When the sun's energy reaches the Earth, some of it is reflected back into space, and the rest is absorbed by the land and oceans. This absorbed energy is then released back as heat. Certain gases in the atmosphere, like carbon dioxide, trap some of this heat. They act like the glass walls of a greenhouse, keeping the Earth warm enough for us to live. This is the natural greenhouse effect, and it's a good thing. However, human activities, like burning fossil fuels (coal, oil, and gas), are releasing too much carbon dioxide into the atmosphere. This is making the greenhouse effect stronger and causing the Earth's temperature to rise. This is often called global warming or climate change.

Vocabulary

  • reflect 반사하다
  • absorb 흡수하다
  • atmosphere 대기
  • fossil fuels 화석 연료
  • climate change 기후 변화
72-1 Question

What is the natural greenhouse effect?

  1. A process that cools the Earth
  2. A process where gases trap heat to keep the Earth warm
  3. A type of house made of glass
  4. A problem caused by too much rain
  5. The changing of the seasons
72-2 Question

How are humans making the greenhouse effect stronger?

  1. By planting more trees
  2. By using solar power
  3. By releasing too much carbon dioxide from burning fossil fuels
  4. By building more greenhouses
  5. By reflecting more sunlight back to space
72-3 Question

위 글에 따르면, '온실 효과'라는 이름이 붙은 이유는 무엇인가?

  1. 온실에서 처음 발견된 현상이라서
  2. 대기 중의 가스들이 온실의 유리벽처럼 열을 가두기 때문에
  3. 주로 온실 작물에 영향을 미치기 때문에
  4. 온실처럼 초록색으로 보이기 때문에
  5. 과학자가 온실에서 살았기 때문에
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 73]

A School Field Day

Our school's field day was last Friday. It wasn't a typical sports day with races and competitions. Instead, the theme was "Cooperation and Fun." We were divided into mixed-grade teams. The first game was called "Human Knot." Our team had to stand in a circle, grab hands with two different people across from us, and then try to untangle ourselves without letting go. It was chaotic but hilarious. Another game was "Pass the Water," where we had to pass a cup of water over our heads using only our feet. We got completely wet but laughed so hard. There were no winners or losers, just teams working together and having a good time. It was a great way to build teamwork and make new friends from different grades.

Vocabulary

  • cooperation 협동, 협력
  • untangle (얽힌 것을) 풀다
  • chaotic 혼돈스러운, 무질서한
  • hilarious 아주 우스운, 재미있는
  • teamwork 팀워크, 협동 작업
73-1 Question

What was the main theme of the field day?

  1. Intense Competition
  2. Individual Achievement
  3. Cooperation and Fun
  4. Academic Excellence
  5. Artistic Expression
73-2 Question

Which game is NOT described in the passage?

  1. Human Knot
  2. Pass the Water
  3. A three-legged race
  4. A game where students got wet
  5. A game that required untangling
73-3 Question

이 운동회를 통해 글쓴이가 얻은 가장 큰 소득은 무엇인가?

  1. 달리기 실력 향상
  2. 1등을 해서 받은 상품
  3. 다른 학년 학생들과 팀워크를 다지고 새로운 친구를 사귄 것
  4. 선생님께 칭찬받은 것
  5. 물에 젖지 않고 게임을 마친 것
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 74]

The Legend of Robin Hood

Robin Hood is a legendary hero from English folklore. The stories say he lived in Sherwood Forest with his group of followers, known as the "Merry Men." Robin Hood was an expert archer. He was famous for "robbing from the rich and giving to the poor." At that time, England was ruled by the cruel Prince John and his greedy sheriff of Nottingham. They taxed the poor people heavily. Robin Hood fought against this injustice. He was an outlaw, but he was a hero to the common people. Whether Robin Hood was a real historical figure or just a legend is still debated. But his story of fighting for justice and helping the needy has been popular for centuries.

Vocabulary

  • folklore 민속, 민간 전승
  • archer 궁수, 활 쏘는 사람
  • greedy 탐욕스러운
  • tax 세금을 부과하다
  • injustice 불의, 부당함
  • outlaw 무법자, 법의 보호를 받지 못하는 사람
74-1 Question

What was Robin Hood famous for?

  1. Being the king of England
  2. Building Sherwood Forest
  3. Robbing from the rich and giving to the poor
  4. Being a cruel sheriff
  5. Writing stories about heroes
74-2 Question

Who were the "Merry Men"?

  1. The soldiers of Prince John
  2. The rich people of Nottingham
  3. Robin Hood's group of followers
  4. A group of famous archers
  5. The poor people who received money
74-3 Question

위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  1. 로빈 후드는 역사적으로 실존했던 인물임이 확실하다.
  2. 로빈 후드는 활을 매우 잘 쏘았다.
  3. 존 왕자는 가난한 사람들에게 매우 친절했다.
  4. 로빈 후드는 부자들을 도와 가난한 사람들을 괴롭혔다.
  5. 로빈 후드는 노팅엄의 보안관이었다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 75]

The Simple Joy of Baking Bread

There is something magical about baking your own bread. It starts with just four simple ingredients: flour, water, yeast, and salt. The process requires patience. You mix the ingredients to form a sticky dough. Then comes the most important part: kneading. You push, pull, and fold the dough until it becomes smooth and elastic. This develops the gluten, which gives the bread its structure. Then, you let the dough rest in a warm place. The yeast works its magic, making the dough rise to double its size. Finally, you bake it. The smell of freshly baked bread that fills the house is one of the best smells in the world. Taking a bite of a warm slice of bread that you made with your own hands is a simple but profound joy.

Vocabulary

  • ingredient 재료, 성분
  • patience 인내심
  • dough 밀가루 반죽
  • knead (반죽을) 이기다, 주무르다
  • profound 깊은, 심오한
75-1 Question

What are the four main ingredients for baking bread mentioned in the passage?

  1. Flour, water, sugar, and butter
  2. Flour, water, yeast, and salt
  3. Flour, milk, eggs, and sugar
  4. Flour, yeast, salt, and oil
  5. Water, yeast, salt, and sugar
75-2 Question

What is the purpose of kneading the dough?

  1. To make the dough smaller
  2. To add more flavor
  3. To develop the gluten and make the dough smooth
  4. To cool down the dough
  5. To mix in the yeast
75-3 Question

이 글에서 빵을 굽는 과정이 '마법 같다'고 표현된 이유는 무엇인가?

  1. 빵이 오븐 안에서 저절로 부풀기 때문에
  2. 간단한 재료들이 인내와 과정을 거쳐 맛있는 빵으로 변신하기 때문에
  3. 빵을 굽는 데 특별한 도구가 필요 없기 때문에
  4. 빵 굽는 냄새가 사람들을 행복하게 만들기 때문에
  5. 빵을 만드는 데 시간이 거의 걸리지 않기 때문에
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 76]

The International Space Station (ISS)

The International Space Station (ISS) is a large spacecraft that orbits the Earth. It is a home and a science laboratory for astronauts from many different countries. The ISS was built as a collaborative project among five space agencies: from the USA, Russia, Japan, Europe, and Canada. It is a symbol of international cooperation. Life on the ISS is very different from life on Earth. Because of microgravity, astronauts float instead of walk. They have to strap themselves down to sleep. Eating is also tricky; food has to be specially packaged. Astronauts on the ISS conduct important experiments in fields like biology, physics, and medicine. What they learn helps us understand more about space and can also have benefits for us here on Earth.

Vocabulary

  • orbit ~의 궤도를 돌다
  • laboratory 실험실
  • astronaut 우주비행사
  • collaborative 협력적인, 공동의
  • cooperation 협력, 협동
  • conduct 수행하다
76-1 Question

What is the International Space Station?

  1. A planet where humans can live
  2. A spacecraft that travels to Mars
  3. A space laboratory and home for astronauts that orbits Earth
  4. A powerful telescope in space
  5. A rocket used for launching satellites
76-2 Question

Why do astronauts on the ISS float?

  1. The air is very thin.
  2. There is no floor.
  3. Because of microgravity.
  4. They wear special floating suits.
  5. It is part of their training.
76-3 Question

위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  1. ISS는 한 국가가 단독으로 만들었다.
  2. ISS는 국제 협력의 상징이다.
  3. 우주비행사들은 ISS에서 과학 실험을 수행한다.
  4. ISS에서의 생활은 지구에서의 생활과 매우 다르다.
  5. 우주비행사들은 잠을 잘 때 몸을 묶어야 한다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 77]

The Power of Positive Thinking

Do you see the glass as half full or half empty? This question is often used to describe two different ways of thinking: optimism and pessimism. Optimists, who see the glass as half full, tend to focus on the good side of things. Pessimists, who see it as half empty, tend to focus on the bad side. Research shows that positive thinking can have a real impact on your life. Optimists are often healthier, less stressed, and more successful in achieving their goals. This is because a positive attitude helps you to be more resilient when you face challenges. It doesn't mean you should ignore problems. It means you should approach them with a hopeful and proactive mindset. Believing in yourself and your ability to succeed is the first step towards success.

Vocabulary

  • optimism 낙관주의
  • pessimism 비관주의
  • impact 영향, 충격
  • attitude 태도
  • resilient 회복력 있는, 탄력 있는
  • proactive 주도적인, 적극적인
77-1 Question

What does it mean to see a glass as "half full"?

  1. To be thirsty
  2. To be a pessimist
  3. To focus on the positive side of things
  4. To want more water
  5. To be realistic
77-2 Question

According to the passage, what is a benefit of being an optimist?

  1. They never have any problems.
  2. They are often healthier and less stressed.
  3. They can predict the future.
  4. They are always happy.
  5. They don't need to work hard.
77-3 Question

이 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?

  1. 긍정적인 사고는 삶에 좋은 영향을 미칠 수 있다.
  2. 비관적인 사람들은 성공할 수 없다.
  3. 문제를 무시하는 것이 행복의 비결이다.
  4. 물을 항상 반 이상 채워 마셔야 한다.
  5. 성공은 오직 운에 달려 있다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 78]

A World Without Bees

Bees are small insects, but they play a huge role in our ecosystem. They are one of the most important pollinators. A pollinator is an animal that moves pollen from one plant to another, which helps the plants produce fruit and seeds. About one-third of the food we eat, including fruits like apples and strawberries, and vegetables like broccoli and cucumbers, depends on pollination by bees. Unfortunately, the world's bee population is in decline. This is due to problems like habitat loss, pesticide use, and climate change. A world without bees would mean a world with much less food. It would be a disaster for both nature and humans. We need to protect these tiny, hardworking creatures.

Vocabulary

  • ecosystem 생태계
  • pollinator 꽃가루 매개자
  • pollen 꽃가루
  • population 개체 수, 인구
  • decline 감소, 하락
  • pesticide 살충제, 농약
78-1 Question

What is a "pollinator"?

  1. An animal that eats plants
  2. An animal that moves pollen between plants
  3. A type of bee
  4. A person who keeps bees
  5. A type of fruit
78-2 Question

Why are bees important for humans?

  1. They produce delicious honey.
  2. They are beautiful to look at.
  3. They help produce a large portion of our food.
  4. They keep other harmful insects away.
  5. They are a symbol of hard work.
78-3 Question

위 글에 따르면, 벌의 개체 수가 감소하는 원인이 아닌 것은?

  1. 서식지 감소
  2. 살충제 사용
  3. 기후 변화
  4. 너무 많은 꿀의 생산
  5. 인간의 활동
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 79]

The Day I Got Lost

When I was eight, my family went to a large, crowded department store. I was holding my mom's hand, but I let go for just a second to look at a toy. When I looked back, she was gone. I looked around, but I couldn't see her anywhere. Panic set in. The store seemed huge and full of strangers. I started to cry. A kind store employee saw me. She knelt down and asked for my name and my mom's name. She spoke in a calm and gentle voice, which made me feel a little better. She took me to the customer service center and made an announcement over the speakers. A few minutes later, my mom came running. She hugged me so tightly. That day, I learned how important it is to stay close to my parents in a crowded place.

Vocabulary

  • department store 백화점
  • panic 극심한 공포, 공황
  • employee 직원
  • announcement 안내 방송, 발표
79-1 Question

Why did the writer get lost?

  1. He ran away from his mom.
  2. He let go of his mom's hand to look at a toy.
  3. His mom left him alone.
  4. He fell asleep in the store.
  5. He went to the wrong floor.
79-2 Question

Who helped the writer find his mom?

  1. A police officer
  2. Another child
  3. A kind store employee
  4. His dad
  5. He found her by himself.
79-3 Question

이 경험을 통해 글쓴이가 배운 교훈으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  1. 백화점은 위험한 곳이다.
  2. 장난감을 사달라고 조르면 안 된다.
  3. 모르는 사람을 절대 따라가면 안 된다.
  4. 붐비는 장소에서는 부모님 곁에 꼭 붙어 있어야 한다.
  5. 울면 문제가 해결된다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 80]

The Art of Listening

We often think that being a good conversationalist means being a good talker. But being a good listener is just as, if not more, important. True listening is not just waiting for your turn to speak. It's an active process. It means paying full attention to the other person. You should make eye contact and nod to show you are following along. Try to understand not just the words they are saying, but also the feelings behind them. Don't interrupt or start thinking about your reply while they are still talking. When you listen well, you make the other person feel respected and understood. This strengthens your relationships and helps you learn new things. It is a skill that can be improved with practice.

Vocabulary

  • conversationalist 대화를 잘하는 사람
  • attention 주의, 주목
  • interrupt 방해하다, 끼어들다
  • reply 대답, 응답
  • practice 연습
80-1 Question

What is the main idea of the passage?

  1. Being a good talker is the most important skill.
  2. The importance and techniques of being a good listener.
  3. How to win an argument.
  4. Why you should always make eye contact.
  5. The difference between talking and speaking.
80-2 Question

According to the passage, what should you NOT do when listening?

  1. Make eye contact
  2. Nod your head
  3. Pay full attention
  4. Interrupt the speaker
  5. Try to understand their feelings
80-3 Question

위 글에서 '진정한 듣기'란 무엇이라고 설명하는가?

  1. 말할 차례를 조용히 기다리는 것
  2. 상대방에게 온전히 집중하는 능동적인 과정
  3. 상대방의 말에서 틀린 점을 찾아내는 것
  4. 대화 중에 내 대답을 미리 생각하는 것
  5. 고개를 계속 끄덕이는 것

[지문 81]

The Magic of Music

Music has a special power that can touch our hearts and souls. It can make us feel happy when we are sad, or calm when we are anxious. Music exists in every culture around the world, though it may sound very different from place to place. Scientists have discovered that listening to music can actually change our brain chemistry. It can release chemicals called endorphins, which make us feel good. Music can also help us remember things better. That's why many children learn the alphabet through songs. Playing a musical instrument is even more beneficial. It improves coordination between our hands and brain. It also teaches discipline and patience. Whether you are listening to classical music, pop songs, or traditional folk music, you are experiencing one of humanity's greatest gifts.

Vocabulary

  • anxious 불안한
  • chemistry 화학, 화학 작용
  • coordination 조정, 협응
  • discipline 규율, 훈련
  • folk music 민속 음악
81-1 Question

What is the main topic of the passage?

  1. Different types of musical instruments
  2. The power and benefits of music
  3. How to become a professional musician
  4. The history of classical music
  5. Why children should learn songs
81-2 Question

According to the passage, how does music affect our brain?

  1. It makes us smarter immediately.
  2. It can change our brain chemistry and release endorphins.
  3. It helps us speak foreign languages.
  4. It makes our brain smaller.
  5. It has no effect on the brain.
81-3 Question

위 글에서 언급된 악기 연주의 효과가 아닌 것은?

  1. 손과 뇌 사이의 협응력을 향상시킨다.
  2. 규율과 인내심을 가르쳐준다.
  3. 키가 더 크게 자라도록 도와준다.
  4. 기억력 향상에 도움이 된다.
  5. 우리를 기분 좋게 만들어준다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 82]

The Story of Pizza

Pizza, now one of the world's most popular foods, has an interesting history. It started in Naples, Italy, in the 18th century. Poor people in Naples needed cheap, portable food that they could eat with their hands. They began putting tomatoes, oil, and garlic on flat bread. This was the first pizza. In 1889, a pizza maker named Raffaele Esposito created a special pizza for Queen Margherita of Italy. He used tomatoes, mozzarella cheese, and basil to represent the colors of the Italian flag: red, white, and green. This became known as Pizza Margherita. Italian immigrants brought pizza to America in the late 1800s. At first, only Italian Americans ate it. But after World War II, American soldiers who had been in Italy brought back a love for pizza. Soon, pizza became popular all over America and then the world.

Vocabulary

  • portable 휴대할 수 있는
  • represent 나타내다, 상징하다
  • immigrant 이민자
  • soldier 군인
82-1 Question

Where and when did pizza originate?

  1. Naples, Italy in the 18th century
  2. Rome, Italy in the 19th century
  3. New York, America in the 20th century
  4. Paris, France in the 17th century
  5. London, England in the 18th century
82-2 Question

What made Pizza Margherita special?

  1. It was the most expensive pizza.
  2. It used ingredients representing the Italian flag colors.
  3. It was the biggest pizza ever made.
  4. It was made by the queen herself.
  5. It was the first pizza with cheese.
82-3 Question

위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  1. 피자는 부유한 사람들을 위해 처음 만들어졌다.
  2. 미국 군인들이 2차 대전 후 피자에 대한 사랑을 가져왔다.
  3. 피자는 처음부터 전 세계적으로 인기가 있었다.
  4. 피자 마르게리타는 프랑스 국기를 상징한다.
  5. 이탈리아 이민자들은 피자를 미국에 가져오지 않았다.
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 83]

My Grandmother's Garden

Every summer, I visit my grandmother who lives in the countryside. She has a beautiful garden behind her house. In her garden, she grows vegetables like tomatoes, carrots, and lettuce, and fruits like strawberries and apples. But my favorite part of her garden is the flower section. She has roses of different colors, sunflowers that grow taller than me, and lavender that smells wonderful. My grandmother taught me how to plant seeds and water them properly. She showed me how to recognize when vegetables are ready to be picked. We would wake up early in the morning to work in the garden together. She always said, "A garden teaches you patience and rewards you with beauty and food." Working in her garden has made me appreciate where our food comes from and how much care it takes to grow it.

Vocabulary

  • sunflower 해바라기
  • lavender 라벤더
  • recognize 알아보다, 인식하다
  • patience 인내심
83-1 Question

What is the writer's favorite part of the grandmother's garden?

  1. The vegetable section
  2. The fruit trees
  3. The flower section
  4. The tool shed
  5. The greenhouse
83-2 Question

What did the grandmother teach the writer?

  1. How to cook vegetables
  2. How to plant seeds and recognize ripe vegetables
  3. How to sell flowers
  4. How to build a greenhouse
  5. How to make flower arrangements

  1. 정원 가꾸기는 쉽고 즐거운 일이다.
  2. 정원에서 일하면 돈을 많이 벌 수 있다.
  3. 정원 가꾸기는 시간과 노력이 필요하지만 그만한 가치가 있다.
  4. 정원에서는 꽃만 키워야 한다.
  5. 정원 일은 할머니만 할 수 있다.

[지문 84]

The Mystery of Dreams

Every night when you sleep, you enter a fascinating world: the world of dreams. Dreams are sequences of images, emotions, and thoughts that happen in your mind while you sleep. Scientists still don't fully understand why we dream, but they have some interesting theories. One theory suggests that dreams help our brain process and organize the information we learned during the day. Another theory says that dreams help us practice dealing with difficult situations. Most dreams occur during a sleep stage called REM (Rapid Eye Movement) sleep. During this time, your eyes move quickly, but your body is paralyzed to prevent you from acting out your dreams. Some people remember their dreams clearly, while others forget them as soon as they wake up. Dreams can be happy, scary, strange, or realistic. They remain one of the great mysteries of the human mind.

Vocabulary

  • sequence 연속, 순서
  • organize 정리하다, 조직하다
  • paralyzed 마비된
  • prevent 방지하다, 막다
  • mystery 수수께끼, 신비
84-1 Question

What are dreams?

  1. Real events that happen while we sleep
  2. Sequences of images, emotions, and thoughts during sleep
  3. Messages from the future
  4. Problems with our brain
  5. Stories we create when we're awake
84-2 Question

According to the passage, when do most dreams occur?

  1. When we first fall asleep
  2. During deep sleep
  3. During REM sleep
  4. Just before we wake up
  5. Throughout the entire night
84-3 Question

위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  1. 꿈은 잠들 때 우리 마음속에서 일어나는 현상이다.
  2. 과학자들은 꿈의 모든 것을 완전히 이해하고 있다.
  3. REM 수면 중에는 우리 몸이 마비된다.
  4. 어떤 사람들은 꿈을 선명히 기억하고, 어떤 사람들은 바로 잊는다.
  5. 꿈은 행복하거나 무섭거나 이상할 수 있다.

[지문 85]

The Helpful Robot

Last week, our science teacher brought a small robot to class. It was about the size of a basketball and had wheels instead of legs. The robot's name was Helper, and it could do many amazing things. It could navigate around the classroom without bumping into desks or students. It had cameras for eyes and could recognize faces. When the teacher asked it to find someone, it would roll over to that person and say hello. Helper could also carry light objects like books or pencils. The most impressive thing was that it could answer simple questions about science. When a student asked, "What is the largest planet?" Helper replied, "Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system." The teacher explained that robots like Helper are becoming more common in hospitals, homes, and offices. They can help people with various tasks and make life easier.

Vocabulary

  • navigate 길을 찾아가다, 항해하다
  • recognize 인식하다, 알아보다
  • impressive 인상적인
  • various 다양한

  1. Navigate around the classroom
  2. Recognize faces
  3. Cook dinner
  4. Carry light objects
  5. Answer simple questions

  1. "Mars is the largest planet."
  2. "Earth is the largest planet."
  3. "Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system."
  4. "I don't know."
  5. "Saturn is the largest planet."

  2. 과학 실험실에서만
  3. 병원, 가정, 사무실 등 다양한 곳에서
  4. 우주 정거장에서만

[지문 86]

The Importance of Recycling

Recycling is the process of turning used materials into new products. It's one of the most important things we can do to protect our environment. When we recycle, we reduce the amount of waste that goes to landfills. We also save natural resources like trees, water, and minerals. For example, recycling one ton of paper can save 17 trees, 7,000 gallons of water, and enough energy to power a home for six months. There are many things you can recycle: paper, plastic bottles, glass jars, aluminum cans, and old electronics. The recycling process usually involves collecting, sorting, and processing the materials. Then they are made into new products. However, recycling is only effective if people participate. We all need to do our part by separating our trash and putting recyclable items in the right bins.

Vocabulary

  • waste 쓰레기, 폐기물
  • landfill 매립지
  • collect 수집하다
  • sort 분류하다
  • process 처리하다, 가공하다
  • participate 참여하다
86-1 Question

What is the main benefit of recycling mentioned in the passage?

  1. Making money from old items
  2. Protecting the environment and saving resources
  3. Creating new jobs
  4. Making trash bins look nicer
  5. Reducing the size of homes
86-2 Question

According to the passage, recycling one ton of paper can save:

  1. 17 trees and 7,000 gallons of water
  2. 10 trees and 5,000 gallons of water
  3. 20 trees and 10,000 gallons of water
  4. 17 trees but no water
  5. 7,000 trees and 17 gallons of water
86-3 Question

재활용이 효과적이 되기 위해 필요한 것은 무엇인가?

  1. 정부의 강력한 법규만 있으면 된다.
  2. 새로운 기술 개발만 하면 된다.
  3. 사람들이 참여해서 쓰레기를 분리하고 올바른 곳에 버려야 한다.
  4. 재활용 공장을 더 많이 지어야 한다.
  5. 재활용 가능한 제품만 생산해야 한다.

[지문 87]

A Message from the Past

While cleaning out my grandfather's attic, I found an old wooden chest. Inside, there were yellowed photographs, old coins, and a bundle of letters tied with a faded ribbon. The letters were written in beautiful handwriting that looked very different from today's writing. As I read them, I realized they were love letters between my grandfather and grandmother from the 1950s. In the letters, my grandfather wrote about his daily life, his hopes for the future, and his deep love for my grandmother. He described going to the movies, dancing at local halls, and saving money to buy her an engagement ring. My grandmother's letters were equally touching. She wrote about waiting for his letters and how happy they made her feel. Reading these letters was like stepping back in time. It made me realize how precious written words can be and how they can preserve our most important memories.

Vocabulary

  • attic 다락방
  • handwriting 손글씨
  • engagement ring 약혼 반지
  • precious 소중한
  • preserve 보존하다

  2. In his grandfather's attic
  3. In the garden
  4. In the garage
  5. In the library

  1. His job and career plans
  2. His daily life, hopes, and love for grandmother
  3. His travels around the world
  4. His opinions about politics
  5. His recipes for cooking

이 경험을 통해 글쓴이가 깨달은 것은 무엇인가?

  2. 편지가 이메일보다 배달이 느리다는 것
  3. 손으로 쓴 글이 얼마나 소중하고 중요한 기억을 보존할 수 있는지
  4. 1950년대가 지금보다 더 좋은 시대였다는 것

Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 88]

The Amazing Journey of Monarch Butterflies

Every year, millions of monarch butterflies make one of nature's most incredible journeys. These delicate insects travel up to 3,000 miles from Canada and the United States to central Mexico. What makes this journey even more amazing is that the butterflies that start the trip are not the same ones that finish it. The journey takes multiple generations. The butterflies that begin the trip in the fall will mate and die along the way. Their children and grandchildren continue the journey. Even more mysterious is how they know where to go. Scientists believe they use the sun, magnetic fields, and even genetic memory to navigate. The butterflies that reach Mexico will spend the winter there, clustered together on trees to stay warm. In the spring, they begin the journey back north, but again, it takes several generations to complete the round trip.

Vocabulary

  • incredible 믿을 수 없는, 놀라운
  • delicate 연약한, 섬세한
  • multiple 다수의, 여러 개의
  • mate 짝짓기하다
  • navigate 길을 찾아가다
88-1 Question

How far do monarch butterflies travel during their migration?

  1. Up to 1,000 miles
  2. Up to 2,000 miles
  3. Up to 3,000 miles
  4. Up to 4,000 miles
  5. Up to 5,000 miles
88-2 Question

What is unusual about the monarch butterfly migration?

  1. It takes only one day to complete.
  2. The same butterflies that start the trip also finish it.
  3. It takes multiple generations to complete the journey.
  4. They travel alone, not in groups.
  5. They only travel during the winter.
88-3 Question

위 글에 따르면, 나비들이 길을 찾는 방법으로 언급되지 않은 것은?

  1. 태양
  2. 자기장
  3. 유전적 기억
  4. GPS 기술
  5. 자연적인 능력

[지문 89]

The School Chess Club

I joined the school chess club three months ago, and it has been an amazing experience. Chess is often called "the game of kings" because it requires strategy, patience, and deep thinking. At first, I only knew the basic moves of each piece. The bishop moves diagonally, the rook moves in straight lines, and the knight moves in an L-shape. Our club teacher, Mr. Kim, taught us advanced strategies like controlling the center of the board and protecting our king. He also taught us famous openings like the "Queen's Gambit" and the "Sicilian Defense." What I love most about chess is that every game is different. You have to think several moves ahead and try to predict what your opponent will do. Playing chess has improved my concentration and problem-solving skills. It has also taught me that sometimes you have to sacrifice something small to gain something bigger.

Vocabulary

  • bishop (체스의) 비숍
  • rook (체스의) 룩
  • knight (체스의) 나이트
  • opening (체스의) 오프닝
  • problem-solving 문제 해결
89-1 Question

Why is chess called "the game of kings"?

  1. Because only kings were allowed to play it
  2. Because it requires strategy, patience, and deep thinking
  3. Because the king is the most important piece
  4. Because it was invented by a king
  5. Because it is very expensive to play
89-2 Question

Which chess piece moves in an L-shape?

  1. Bishop
  2. Rook
  3. Knight
  4. Queen
  5. Pawn
89-3 Question

체스를 통해 글쓴이가 배운 것이 아닌 것은?

  1. 집중력 향상
  2. 문제 해결 능력 향상
  3. 몇 수 앞을 내다보는 능력
  4. 작은 것을 희생해서 큰 것을 얻는 지혜
  5. 상대방과 빨리 친해지는 방법
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 90]

The Science of Rainbows

A rainbow is one of nature's most beautiful phenomena. But do you know how rainbows form? It all starts with sunlight, which looks white but actually contains all the colors of the spectrum: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet. When sunlight passes through water droplets in the air, something magical happens. The water acts like a tiny prism, splitting the white light into its component colors. This process is called refraction. For you to see a rainbow, the sun must be behind you and there must be water droplets in front of you. That's why rainbows often appear after it rains, when the sun comes out while there are still water droplets in the air. The colors always appear in the same order, with red on the outside and violet on the inside. Sometimes you can see a double rainbow, which happens when light is reflected twice inside the water droplets.

Vocabulary

  • phenomenon 현상 (복수: phenomena)
  • spectrum 스펙트럼, 빛의 띠
  • prism 프리즘
  • refraction 굴절
  • double rainbow 이중 무지개
90-1 Question

What causes a rainbow to form?

  1. Only sunlight
  2. Only rain
  3. Sunlight passing through water droplets and splitting into colors
  4. Reflection from the ground
  5. Magic in the sky
90-2 Question

What must be true for you to see a rainbow?

  1. The sun must be in front of you
  2. It must be nighttime
  3. The sun must be behind you and there must be water droplets in front
  4. There must be clouds everywhere
  5. It must be winter

무지개의 색깔 순서에서 가장 바깥쪽과 가장 안쪽의 색은?

  1. 바깥쪽: 보라색, 안쪽: 빨간색
  2. 바깥쪽: 빨간색, 안쪽: 보라색
  3. 바깥쪽: 파란색, 안쪽: 노란색
  4. 바깥쪽: 초록색, 안쪽: 주황색
  5. 바깥쪽: 노란색, 안쪽: 파란색
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 91]

The Art of Origami

Origami, the Japanese art of paper folding, is much more than just a hobby. The word "origami" comes from two Japanese words: "ori" meaning "to fold" and "kami" meaning "paper." This ancient art form requires only one material: a square piece of paper. No scissors, glue, or tape are allowed. Despite this limitation, origami artists can create incredibly complex and beautiful sculptures. From simple cranes and flowers to detailed dragons and geometric shapes, the possibilities are endless. Origami teaches important life skills. It requires precision, patience, and the ability to follow detailed instructions. It also helps develop spatial reasoning and fine motor skills. Many schools now use origami in math classes because it helps students understand geometry and fractions. The meditative nature of folding paper can also reduce stress and improve focus. What starts as a flat piece of paper becomes a three-dimensional work of art through the magic of folding.

Vocabulary

  • material 재료
  • complex 복잡한
  • precision 정확성, 정밀함
  • reasoning 추론, 논리적 사고
  • meditative 명상적인
91-1 Question

What does the word "origami" mean?

  1. Paper cutting
  2. Paper folding
  3. Paper painting
  4. Paper tearing
  5. Paper gluing
91-2 Question

What materials are allowed in traditional origami?

  1. Paper, scissors, and glue
  2. Only a square piece of paper
  3. Paper and tape
  4. Colored paper and markers
  5. Any kind of paper and tools
91-3 Question

종이접기가 학교에서 수학 수업에 사용되는 이유는?

  1. 종이가 싸기 때문에
  2. 학생들이 기하학과 분수를 이해하는 데 도움이 되기 때문에
  3. 교실을 조용하게 만들기 때문에
  4. 예술 작품을 만들 수 있기 때문에
  5. 종이를 아낄 수 있기 때문에

[지문 92]

A Day at the Science Museum

Last Saturday, my family visited the city science museum. It was much more exciting than I had expected. The museum had interactive exhibits where we could touch and experiment with different scientific concepts. In the physics section, I played with a giant pendulum and learned how it always swings back to the same height. The chemistry lab let visitors do safe experiments, like creating erupting volcanoes with baking soda and vinegar. My favorite part was the planetarium, where we watched a show about the solar system. The narrator explained how vast the universe is and showed us beautiful images of distant galaxies. We also visited the dinosaur hall, where a huge T-Rex skeleton stood in the center. The museum had many hands-on activities that made learning fun and memorable. I left the museum feeling inspired to learn more about science.

Vocabulary

  • exhibit 전시물
  • pendulum 진자, 추
  • erupt 분출하다
  • narrator 내레이터, 해설자
  • galaxy 은하
92-1 Question

What made the science museum special?

  1. It was very old
  2. It had interactive exhibits where visitors could experiment
  3. It was free for everyone
  4. It was the largest museum in the city
  5. It only had dinosaur bones
92-2 Question

What did the writer learn about the pendulum?

  2. It always swings back to the same height
  3. It can tell time
  4. It is dangerous to touch
  5. It was invented yesterday

  2. 화학 실험실
  3. 천체 투영관
  4. 공룡 전시관

[지문 93]

The Power of Kindness

Small acts of kindness can make a big difference in someone's day. Last week, I witnessed a beautiful example of this. I was waiting for the bus when I saw an elderly woman struggling with heavy grocery bags. She looked tired and was having difficulty managing all the bags. A young man nearby noticed her situation. Without hesitation, he approached her and offered to help. "Let me carry those for you," he said with a warm smile. The woman's face lit up with gratitude. "Thank you so much, dear," she replied. The young man carried her bags to the bus stop and even helped her get on the bus. The entire interaction took only a few minutes, but the impact was tremendous. The woman seemed much happier, and other people at the bus stop were smiling too. It reminded me that kindness is contagious – when we see someone being kind, it inspires us to be kind as well.

Vocabulary

  • manage 처리하다, 다루다
  • hesitation 주저함, 망설임
  • gratitude 감사함
  • tremendous 엄청난, 거대한
  • contagious 전염성이 있는
93-1 Question

What problem did the elderly woman have?

  2. She was struggling with heavy grocery bags
  3. She missed her bus
  4. She lost her wallet
  5. She felt sick

How did the young man help the elderly woman?

  3. He carried her bags and helped her get on the bus

  2. 친절한 행동을 보면 다른 사람들도 친절해지고 싶어진다는 뜻

[지문 94]

The History of the Olympic Games

The Olympic Games have a long and fascinating history that dates back to ancient Greece. The first ancient Olympics were held in Olympia, Greece, in 776 BC. These games were held every four years for nearly 12 centuries. The ancient Olympics were quite different from today's games. Only men could compete, and they often competed naked. The games included events like running, wrestling, boxing, and chariot racing. The Olympics were so important that all wars stopped during the games, a period called the "Olympic Truce." The ancient Olympics ended around 400 AD when the Roman Emperor banned them. The modern Olympics began in 1896 when a French educator named Pierre de Coubertin revived the games. He believed that sports could promote peace and understanding between nations. Today, the Olympics include both Summer and Winter games, and athletes from all over the world participate.

Vocabulary

  • ancient 고대의
  • compete 경쟁하다, 출전하다
  • naked 벌거벗은
  • educator 교육자
  • revive 되살리다, 부활시키다
94-1 Question

When and where were the first ancient Olympics held?

  1. Athens, Greece in 776 BC
  2. Olympia, Greece in 776 BC
  3. Rome, Italy in 776 BC
  4. Sparta, Greece in 776 BC
  5. Troy, Greece in 776 BC
94-2 Question

What was the "Olympic Truce"?

  1. A special event in the games
  2. A period when all wars stopped during the Olympics
  3. The prize given to winners
  4. The oath taken by athletes
  5. The opening ceremony

고대 올림픽과 현대 올림픽의 차이점이 아닌 것은?

  2. 고대에는 선수들이 벌거벗고 경기했다
  3. 고대에는 4년마다 열렸다
  4. 고대에는 겨울 경기가 없었다

[지문 95]

My First Job Interview

Last month, I applied for a part-time job at a local bookstore. When they called me for an interview, I was both excited and nervous. I had never been to a job interview before. My mom helped me prepare by practicing common interview questions. She told me to dress professionally and arrive early. On the day of the interview, I put on my best shirt and pants and arrived 15 minutes early. The manager, Ms. Lee, was very friendly. She asked me about my experience with books and why I wanted to work there. I explained that I love reading and that I thought working in a bookstore would be perfect for me. She also asked about my availability and whether I could work weekends. The interview lasted about 20 minutes. At the end, Ms. Lee said she would call me in a few days with her decision. Three days later, I got the call – I got the job! I was thrilled and couldn't wait to start working.

Vocabulary

  • professionally 전문적으로
  • manager 매니저, 관리자
  • experience 경험
  • availability 가능한 시간, 여유

  4. He asked his friends for advice
  5. He didn't prepare at all

  2. About his experience with books and his availability
  3. About his grades in school
  4. About his future career plans

  2. 집에서 가까워서

[지문 96]

The Mystery of Sleep

We spend about one-third of our lives sleeping, yet sleep remains one of the greatest mysteries of human existence. While we sleep, our bodies may look inactive, but our brains are actually very busy. Sleep occurs in different stages, each serving important functions. During deep sleep, our bodies repair tissues and strengthen our immune system. During REM (Rapid Eye Movement) sleep, our brains process memories and emotions. Scientists have discovered that lack of sleep can have serious consequences. People who don't get enough sleep have trouble concentrating, making decisions, and controlling their emotions. Chronic sleep deprivation can even lead to health problems like obesity, diabetes, and heart disease. Different people need different amounts of sleep, but most adults need 7-9 hours per night. Creating a good sleep routine, such as going to bed at the same time each night and avoiding screens before bedtime, can help improve sleep quality.

Vocabulary

  • mystery 수수께끼, 신비
  • inactive 비활성의, 움직이지 않는
  • stage 단계, 단계
  • immune system 면역 체계
  • consequence 결과, 영향
  • deprivation 부족, 결핍
96-1 Question

What happens to our brain while we sleep?

  1. It becomes completely inactive
  2. It becomes very busy processing memories and repairing the body
  3. It stops working entirely
  4. It only dreams
  5. It shrinks in size
96-2 Question

What are some consequences of not getting enough sleep?

  1. Better concentration and decision-making
  2. Improved emotional control
  3. Trouble concentrating, making decisions, and controlling emotions
  4. Increased energy levels
  5. Better health overall
96-3 Question

좋은 수면 습관으로 언급된 것은?

  1. 매일 다른 시간에 잠자리에 들기
  2. 잠자기 전에 스크린 보기
  3. 매일 같은 시간에 잠자리에 들고 잠자기 전 스크린 피하기

  5. 낮에 많이 자기
Vera's Flavor편입영어 독해 Basic 편
[지문 97]

The Story of Chocolate Chip Cookies

One of America's favorite treats, the chocolate chip cookie, was actually invented by accident. In 1938, Ruth Wakefield was running the Toll House Inn in Massachusetts. She was preparing chocolate cookies for her guests when she realized she was out of baker's chocolate. Instead, she broke up a bar of semi-sweet chocolate and added the pieces to her cookie dough, expecting them to melt and create chocolate cookies. However, the chocolate pieces held their shape, creating the world's first chocolate chip cookies. The guests loved them so much that Ruth began serving them regularly. Word spread about these amazing cookies, and soon people were asking for the recipe. Ruth eventually sold the recipe to Nestlé chocolate company in exchange for a lifetime supply of chocolate. The Toll House Chocolate Chip Cookie became so popular that it is now considered an American classic. Today, billions of chocolate chip cookies are eaten around the world every year.

Vocabulary

  • accident 우연, 사고
  • baker's chocolate 제빵용 초콜릿
  • semi-sweet 세미 스위트의 (약간 단)
  • dough 반죽
  • eventually 결국, 마침내
97-1 Question

How were chocolate chip cookies invented?

  1. Ruth Wakefield planned to create them
  2. They were invented by a famous chef
  3. Ruth accidentally created them when she ran out of baker's chocolate
  4. A chocolate company invented them
  5. They were brought from Europe
97-2 Question

What did Ruth Wakefield expect to happen when she added chocolate pieces?

  1. She expected them to stay in pieces
  2. She expected them to melt and create chocolate cookies
  3. She expected them to disappear
  4. She expected them to change color
  5. She expected nothing special

  2. 평생 초콜릿을 공급받는 조건으로 레시피를 팔았다
  3. 레시피를 공짜로 주었다
  4. 네슬레와 공동으로 사업을 시작했다
  5. 네슬레에서 일하기 시작했다

[지문 98]

The Magic of Libraries

Libraries are much more than just buildings filled with books. They are gateways to knowledge, imagination, and community. In ancient times, libraries were only for the wealthy and scholars. The famous Library of Alexandria in Egypt was one of the largest libraries in the ancient world. Today, public libraries serve everyone in the community. Modern libraries offer much more than books. You can borrow DVDs, use computers, attend workshops, and participate in reading programs. Many libraries have makerspaces where people can use 3D printers, learn coding, or work on craft projects. Libraries also serve as community centers where people can meet, study, and attend cultural events. For many people, libraries provide free access to information and technology that they might not otherwise have. The librarians are trained to help you find exactly what you need, whether it's a specific book, research information, or guidance on using technology. In our digital age, libraries continue to evolve and remain vital community resources.

Vocabulary

  • gateway 관문, 입구
  • scholar 학자
  • workshop 워크숍, 강습회
  • makerspace 메이커스페이스 (창작 공간)
  • librarian 사서
  • evolve 발전하다, 진화하다
98-1 Question

How have libraries changed from ancient times to today?

  1. They have become smaller
  2. They were only for wealthy people and scholars, but now serve everyone
  3. They only contain books now
  4. They are less important now
  5. They are exactly the same
98-2 Question

What services do modern libraries offer besides books?

  1. Only computer access
  2. DVDs, computers, workshops, makerspaces, and community events
  3. Only reading programs
  4. Just a quiet place to study
  5. Only internet access

현대 도서관이 지역 사회에서 중요한 이유로 언급되지 않은 것은?

  2. 지역 사회 센터 역할을 한다
  3. 다양한 문화 행사를 제공한다
  4. 최신 영화를 무료로 상영해준다
  5. 전문 사서가 도움을 제공한다

[지문 99]

Learning a Foreign Language

Learning a foreign language is one of the most rewarding challenges you can take on. When I started learning Spanish two years ago, I thought it would be impossible. The grammar seemed complicated, and the pronunciation was difficult. However, my teacher gave me some helpful advice. She said, "Don't be afraid to make mistakes. Mistakes are how we learn." She encouraged me to practice speaking with classmates, even if I wasn't perfect. I also started watching Spanish movies with subtitles and listening to Spanish music. Gradually, I began to understand more and more. Now, I can have simple conversations in Spanish, and I'm planning to visit Mexico next summer to practice with native speakers. Learning Spanish has opened up a new world for me. I can communicate with millions more people, understand different cultures better, and it even looks good on college applications. Most importantly, it has given me confidence that I can learn anything if I put my mind to it.

Vocabulary

  • rewarding 보람 있는
  • challenge 도전
  • grammar 문법
  • pronunciation 발음
  • subtitle 자막

  2. Don't be afraid to make mistakes
  3. Only speak when you're perfect
  4. Don't talk to other students
  5. Focus only on reading

  3. By traveling to Spain immediately
  4. By taking more classes

[지문 100]

The End of Summer

As I sit by my bedroom window watching the leaves begin to change colors, I realize that summer is coming to an end. It feels like just yesterday that school finished and the long, sunny days of vacation began. This summer was filled with so many memorable experiences. I went camping with my family, learned to swim, read five novels, and made new friends at summer camp. I also accomplished something I never thought I could do – I learned to ride a bicycle without training wheels. My dad spent hours running alongside me in the park until I finally got the hang of it. Now, as September approaches, I have mixed feelings. I'm sad to see the warm weather and freedom of summer go, but I'm also excited about starting a new school year. I'll be in a new grade with new teachers and new subjects to learn. I'm curious about what challenges and adventures await me. Summer taught me that time passes quickly, so it's important to appreciate every moment and try new things. I'm grateful for all the wonderful memories I made.

Vocabulary

  • memorable 기억에 남는
  • accomplish 성취하다
  • curious 궁금한, 호기심 있는
  • adventure 모험
  • appreciate 감사하다, 소중히 여기다
100-1 Question

What new skill did the writer learn this summer?

  1. How to swim
  2. How to ride a bicycle without training wheels
  3. How to read novels
  4. How to make friends
  5. How to change colors

How does the writer feel about the new school year?

  1. Only excited
  2. Only sad
  3. Mixed feelings – sad about summer ending but excited about new challenges
  4. Worried and scared
  5. Indifferent
